sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.125 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a finite group admitting a regular elementary abelian group of automorphisms$V$of order$p^n$. Is it true that the subgroup$H= _{v V \{ 1\}} [G,v]$is nilpotent? In the case of an affirmative answer, does there exist a function depending only on$p$,$n$, and the derived length of$G$which bounds the nilpotency class of$H$? }

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
